demographics.living_situation: lives alone in a private residence

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: I am passing pure water all day and all night, I haven't slept properly in weeks, and I feel completely drained of all energy.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: The watery diarrhoea just won't stop — I'm up going to the toilet all night, I'm exhausted, and I'm starting to feel a bit shaky and dizzy when I stand up.

symptom_narrative.onset: about two months ago, and it has just kept going without letting up
symptom_narrative.character: completely watery, like pure liquid — no blood and nothing that looks like pus or mucus
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: up to about ten times a day and night; the worst thing is being woken over and over so I never really sleep
symptom_narrative.location_as_patient_describes: just a mild all-over crampy tummy discomfort — nothing sharp or in one spot
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant, and it wakes me several times every night to rush to the toilet
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing I have tried seems to help
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: it happens no matter what I eat or do
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: I have to go so urgently that I have had a couple of accidents, and I have lost about 3 kilos without trying. I feel wrung out and a bit lightheaded when I get up quickly.
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: I have never had anything like this before it started two months ago
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: I am so worn out I am moving around slowly, and I am frightened to leave the house in case I cannot get to a toilet in time

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: an underactive/autoimmune thyroid problem (Hashimoto's) that I have known about for years
arthritis in my joints that gives me a lot of pain
past_medical_history.how_managed: I manage the pain myself with tablets from the chemist

current_medications_as_reported.name_as_patient_states: ibuprofen tablets from the chemist for my joint pain
current_medications_as_reported.dose_as_patient_reports: 400 mg, about three times a day, and I have been taking them daily for the past year

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: lives alone; worried about falling if she gets dizzy, and about not being able to reach the bathroom in time

objective_data_offered.value: about 65 kg now, down roughly 3 kg over the last two months
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: self-reported from home scales; weight loss estimated by the patient, not measured serially

objective_data_offered.value: feels shaky and lightheaded for a few seconds when standing up quickly, especially getting up at night
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: a reported sensation, not a measured blood-pressure change
